title: Keinemusik Festival
articleDescription: Extra services to and from the Flemington Racecourse on Thursday 2 April 2026.
articleContent:
Extra services to and from the Flemington Racecourse on Thursday 2 April.

Before you travel

If you use Journey Planner, you'll see all the extra services we've added to help you get there.

Metropolitan train

Getting to and from Flemington Racecourse

Trains will operate from Flinders Street (Platforms 9/10) in both directions, stopping at Southern Cross (Platforms 13/14) and North Melbourne (Platforms 5/6), then express to Flemington Racecourse (Platform 1).

Trains will commence from Flinders Street towards Flemington Racecourse at 7.20pm. 
The final train departing Flemington Racecourse towards Flinders Street will be at 12.11am on Friday 3 April.

Metropolitan tram

Getting to and from Flemington Racecourse

Extra Trams will operate via Route 57a via Flemington Racecourse, between Stop 01 Flinders Street / Elizabeth Street and Stop 40 Epsom Road/ Maribyrnon Road, for patrons wishing to make their way to and from the event. 

Passengers may alight and board trams at Stop 30 Flemington Racecourse on Epsom Road. 

Metropolitan bus

Getting to and from Flemington Racecourse

The following bus route travels close to Flemington Racecourse: 

  • Route 472 (Williamstown to Moonee Ponds via Footscray) (please note service may finish prior to the end of the music festival)

Passengers can catch buses from Footscray Station and alight at Langs Road/Showgrounds. 

Regional train

Getting to and from Flemington Racecourse

Passengers arriving at Southern Cross station are advised to change trains and catch a Flemington Racecourse service. Services will return then via Southern Cross Station.

Refer to the Metropolitan trains section above for platform information. 

Additional information

  • Visitors to Victoria: Here for a special event? Read more about our public transport system.
  • Help and support: View popular public transport topics, including ticketing.
  • Accessible travel: View resources for people with accessibility needs on public transport.
  • VicTraffic: Road closures, bushfire activity and other events on Victoria’s roads may affect your journey. Plan a safer trip by viewing real-time alerts on VicTraffic.
  • On public holidays, daily myki fare caps apply ($8 full fare and $4 concession/senior), giving you unlimited travel for the day.
  • For more ticketing information, including myki, regional travel and fare caps, visit: Tickets and myki – Transport Victoria.
